scatter.why: Documents were embedded with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, projected with UMAP, and grouped by BERTopic (HDBSCAN density clustering + c-TF-IDF keyword labeling). Points that sit close together share semantic content in abstracts and titles.

0.explanation: Plateau: volume stabilizes near the S-curve peak; incremental improvements dominate.

1.explanation: Plateau: volume stabilizes near the S-curve peak; incremental improvements dominate.

events.why: Topic 0 first appears in 2010 with 1 document(s); keyword overlap vs prior windows was zero.
events.llm_explanation: Based on corpus patterns, this shift likely reflects converging research interest and cross-pollination between adjacent technology areas. Competing approaches may consolidate over the next five years as standards emerge.

evolution.why: Per-topic yearly counts were fitted to a logistic S-curve to assign lifecycle stages. Change events are detected by diffing topic presence and volume across years and measuring keyword overlap between co-active clusters.

influence.reason: Shared methodological lineage between the, of, to, and and the, of, in, and in the corpus.
influence.why: Heuristic edge from cluster ordering when LLM output was sparse.

0.why: spaCy dependency parsing extracted Subject–Action–Object patterns from abstracts; dominant verbs summarize what each cluster *does* technologically.

1.why: spaCy dependency parsing extracted Subject–Action–Object patterns from abstracts; dominant verbs summarize what each cluster *does* technologically.

reasoning.why_influence: Edges represent enabling or displacing relationships inferred from cluster themes and LLM foresight reasoning.

drivers.description: 2 emerging topic(s) create uncertainty about which technology will cross the chasm first.
drivers.why: Derived from emerge events—new clusters increase adoption-path uncertainty.

drivers.description: 1 declining topic(s) signal potential displacement of incumbent approaches.
drivers.why: Derived from die events—fading clusters imply replacement risk for legacy tech.

optimistic.title: Optimistic
optimistic.narrative: Based on corpus patterns, this shift likely reflects converging research interest and cross-pollination between adjacent technology areas. Competing approaches may consolidate over the next five years as standards emerge.
optimistic.why: Narrative generated for high Adoption velocity × high Technology displacement quadrant using cluster themes and detected evolution events.

disruptive.title: Disruptive
disruptive.narrative: Based on corpus patterns, this shift likely reflects converging research interest and cross-pollination between adjacent technology areas. Competing approaches may consolidate over the next five years as standards emerge.
disruptive.why: Narrative generated for high Adoption velocity × low Technology displacement quadrant using cluster themes and detected evolution events.

constrained.title: Constrained
constrained.narrative: Based on corpus patterns, this shift likely reflects converging research interest and cross-pollination between adjacent technology areas. Competing approaches may consolidate over the next five years as standards emerge.
constrained.why: Narrative generated for low Adoption velocity × high Technology displacement quadrant using cluster themes and detected evolution events.

stagnant.title: Stagnant

scenarios.why: Four scenarios span the 2×2 space of the two largest uncertainty drivers extracted from causal events.

tree.name: The
tree.why: Primary driver in the Optimistic scenario.

children.name: Supply chain shift
children.why: Component demand reallocates toward new dominant designs.

optimistic.why: D3 impact tree encodes cascading second-order effects implied by the scenario narrative.
